author: Dr. Marcus Hong (PGY-3) / Dr. Jennifer Beckman (Medicine attending)
content:
DAILY PROGRESS NOTE — 3/20/2027, 9:15 AM (Hospital Day 34, Floor day 8)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Location: 5N bed 14
Interpreter: Vietnamese phone interpreter used for bedside discussion.

24-HOUR EVENTS:
Uneventful overnight. Slept reasonably (4-5 hour stretches, with 1 brief wakening for restroom).
- PT (yesterday afternoon): Ambulated 75 feet with rolling walker and contact-guard assist (improved from 50 feet 2 days ago). Patient tolerated well; SpO2 maintained 95% RA throughout.
- OT (yesterday): Working on ADLs — patient now able to dress upper body and brush teeth independently. Lower-body dressing requires moderate assistance.
- Tolerating regular diet at 70-80% intake; nutrition consult continuing to monitor.
- DVT: no new symptoms; right calf without tenderness, mild residual swelling (1+) unchanged.
- No bleeding events.
- No fevers, no respiratory complaints.
- Family visited yesterday afternoon for ~3 hours.

SYSTEMS:

NEURO: Alert and oriented x 3 via interpreter. Sleeping well. Mood appropriate; says she 'wants to go home or to rehab.'

RESPIRATORY: On room air, SpO2 96-97%. No work of breathing.

CARDIOVASCULAR: BP 132/78 today; well-controlled on lisinopril 5 mg. HR 80, sinus rhythm.

GI / NUTRITION: Tolerating regular diet ~70-80% intake. Nutrition consult yesterday recommended Ensure supplement BID between meals to address ICU-related weight loss. Pancreatitis-related symptoms resolved.

RENAL: Cr stable at 1.3. UOP adequate. Holding NSAIDs.

ID: Off all antibiotics x 12 days. Afebrile throughout. No new infectious concerns.

HEME: Apixaban 5 mg BID, day 18 of treatment. Hgb 9.6 today (up from nadir 7.4 post-ICU). No bleeding.

MSK / FUNCTIONAL: Improving with PT/OT but still significantly deconditioned. Cannot yet ambulate safely without assistance. Anticipated 2-3 more weeks of structured rehab needed.

PSYCH / SOCIAL: Mood good; engaged. Family supportive. Wants to discuss when she can go home — discussed need for acute rehab placement; she understands but expresses preference for home as a future goal.

PROBLEMS / PLAN:
#1 Severe acute biliary pancreatitis, resolved.
#2 RLE DVT — on apixaban 5 mg BID; 3-month minimum course; hematology follow-up arranged for 1 month post-discharge.
#3 Cholelithiasis — General Surgery (Dr. Markovich) consulted again 3/18; recommended elective cholecystectomy 6-8 weeks post-discharge after recovery. Will not pursue inpatient.
#4 ICU-acquired weakness / severe deconditioning — primary driver of remaining hospitalization. Continued PT/OT 2x/day. Aiming for acute rehab placement.
#5 CKD stage 3a — at baseline.
#6 HTN — controlled.
#7 HLD — at goal.
#8 LTBI — known, no changes.
#9 Anemia — chronic + ICU; trending up; transfusion not indicated.
#10 Nutrition — Ensure BID added; will reassess weekly.

DISCHARGE PLANNING UPDATE:
- Case management has received acceptance from Bayview Acute Rehabilitation (3/19) — bed available expected 4/2-4/5 (~2 weeks).
- Anticipated total LOS: ~7 more weeks then discharge to home.
- Family-meeting scheduled for 3/23 with case management, physical therapy, social work, and son Quang (via Vietnamese interpreter) — to plan logistics of rehab transition.

FAMILY UPDATE TODAY: Son Quang updated via interpreter; comfortable with plan. Asked about pancreatitis recurrence risk — answered that the gallbladder cholecystectomy at 6-8 weeks post-discharge will significantly reduce risk; counseled on dietary precautions until then. Asked about long-term anticoagulation — answered with hematology plan and 3-month re-evaluation.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Marcus Hong, MD (PGY-3)
Dr. Jennifer Beckman, MD (Attending)
Date: 3/20/2027
